title: [v4.0-rhel] Do not error on signalling a just-stopped container
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #14533

/assign lsm5

```release-note
Fixed a bug where Podman could print error messages when signals were forwarded to a container via `--sig-proxy` to a container as the container process exited.
```

comments.body: LGTM
/approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *openshift-cherrypick-robot*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v4.0-rhel/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Lot of netavark test failures, looking...

```

Downloading latest netavark from upstream branch 'v1.0.1-rhel'
  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
                                 Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed

  0     0    0     0    0     0      0      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--     0
100    22  100    22    0     0     44      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--    44
100    22  100    22    0     0     44      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--    44
/usr/local/libexec/podman
Archive:  /tmp/netavark.zip
warning [/tmp/netavark.zip]:  zipfile is empty
```

Potentially a network issue (on the Github or Cirrus end)? Or maybe that release just disappeared...

Restarting one of the failures to see if it's a flake

/lgtm
/hold

In case it is
Seems to fail consistently
@lsm5 what's up with this PR?  Do we need it? If it's for RHEL please link a BZ.
https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2097049
@mheon do you know what's up with CI?
Potentially trying to grab a bad version of Netavark for this branch? @cevich PTAL

Looking...

```
Downloading latest netavark from upstream branch 'v1.0.1-rhel'
  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
                                 Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed

  0     0    0     0    0     0      0      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--     0
100    22  100    22    0     0     68      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--    68
/usr/local/libexec/podman
Archive:  /tmp/netavark.zip
warning [/tmp/netavark.zip]:  zipfile is empty
```

...sigh, the netavark CI VM images got pruned :sob: The cirrus-cron jobs have been failing since June and nobody noticed because monitoring didn't exist.  The monitoring part is fixed by https://github.com/containers/netavark/pull/325 but it may be too late for me to recover the VM images, still looking...
...well If I can't be smart all the time, at least I can be lucky: https://github.com/containers/netavark/pull/337  <-- should fix this after it merges and CI runs on that branch.
@cevich Does this need a rebase?
Looking.

`warning [/tmp/netavark.zip]:  zipfile is empty`

This is referring to `https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/github/containers/netavark/success/binary.zip?branch=v1.0.1-rhel`.  I suspect something changed on that branch which is causing the netavark binary not to be included for some reason.  Once that's fixed, re-running the jobs here should pick it up.  Investigating...

...curious, it appears a [recent job run by cirrus-cron on the `v1.0.1-rhel` branch successfully built and stored all the proper artifacts](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5203389763026944?logs=binary#L0-L3).  Though there was a problem early last week, everything looks good now.  Let me try re-running a task here...
...looks like it's working now.  Just needed to re-run the jobs (I just did).
https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2097049 targets v4.1.1 where it's fixed.  Please reopen if I am mistaken.
